findings.description: `cut -d'=' -f2` truncates SERVICE_ROLE keys containing `=` padding characters. JWT service role keys often end with `=` or `==`. Should be `cut -d'=' -f2-` (with trailing dash) to capture the full value, matching the SUPABASE_URL extraction on line 53.

The `restore` subcommand still defaults to `postgresql://postgres:postgres@127.0.0.1:54322/postgres` when DATABASE_URL is not set. This contradicts the staging-only migration — direct script invocation bypasses the Makefile guard and silently connects to a non-existent local DB. Should require DATABASE_URL explicitly or remove the localhost default.

The 'Environment Debugging Loops' entry still says `.env.local pointing to remote instance instead of 127.0.0.1:54321` — this is backwards for staging-first development. Should be updated to reflect that the correct target is now the staging Supabase project URL, not localhost.

The `setup` target runs `supabase db push` without first checking if the Supabase CLI is linked (`.supabase/project-ref` exists). If the user skips the link step printed above, db push will fail with an unclear error. Consider adding a guard or making the link step mandatory.

skipped.reason: Empty string default for supabase_url is consistent with existing pattern for other credential fields. Backend fails at first Supabase call which is adequate feedback.
Pre-existing pattern — apikey: placeholder works correctly for Supabase REST health check endpoints and is standard practice.
